fsm_model.section_ref: Matter Specification R1.4 Core Specification, Section 4.14.1 (pages 164-171), Section 3.10 (pages 81-85)

states.name: Idle
states.description: Initial state before PASE protocol initiation. No session establishment in progress.

states.name: I_AwaitingPBKDFParamResponse
states.description: Initiator has sent PBKDFParamRequest, awaiting PBKDFParamResponse from responder

states.name: R_ProcessingPBKDFParamRequest
states.description: Responder received PBKDFParamRequest, validating and preparing response

states.name: R_AwaitingPake1
states.description: Responder has sent PBKDFParamResponse, awaiting Pake1 message

states.name: I_ProcessingPBKDFParamResponse
states.description: Initiator received PBKDFParamResponse, computing PAKE values and pA

states.name: I_AwaitingPake2
states.description: Initiator has sent Pake1 with pA, awaiting Pake2 with pB and cB

states.name: R_ProcessingPake1
states.description: Responder received Pake1, computing pB, TT, cA, cB, and Ke

states.name: R_AwaitingPake3
states.description: Responder has sent Pake2 with pB and cB, awaiting Pake3 with cA for verification

states.name: I_ProcessingPake2
states.description: Initiator received Pake2, computing TT, cA, cB, Ke and verifying cB

states.name: I_AwaitingPakeFinished
states.description: Initiator has sent Pake3 with cA, awaiting PakeFinished status report

states.name: R_ProcessingPake3
states.description: Responder received Pake3, verifying cA confirmation value

states.name: R_SessionEstablished
states.description: Responder has verified cA, sent PakeFinished, session keys installed

states.name: I_SessionEstablished
states.description: Initiator received PakeFinished, session keys installed, ready for encrypted communication

states.name: I_Error_InvalidParameter
states.description: Initiator detected invalid parameter (cB verification failed)

states.name: R_Error_InvalidParameter
states.description: Responder detected invalid parameter (passcodeID or cA verification failed)

states.name: R_Busy
states.description: Responder is unable to fulfill session request, returns BUSY status

transitions.specification_gap: GAP_001: Spec does NOT require initiator to validate PBKDF parameters (iterations, salt length). See Page 169 - no validation steps before Pake1.

functions.name: Crypto_DRBG
functions.description: Deterministic Random Bit Generator that produces cryptographically secure random bytes
functions.algorithm_detail: NIST SP 800-90A compliant DRBG. For PASE, generates 256 bits (32 bytes) of randomness for InitiatorRandom and ResponderRandom.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T01, T05: Generate random values for protocol freshness

functions.name: generate_unique_session_id
functions.description: Generates a unique session identifier that does not overlap with existing PASE or CASE sessions
functions.algorithm_detail: Selects a 16-bit session identifier not currently in use. SHALL NOT overlap with any existing session identifier per Section 4.13.2.4.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T01, T05: Create InitiatorSessionId and ResponderSessionId

functions.name: set_local_session_identifier
functions.description: Sets the Local Session Identifier in the Session Context
functions.algorithm_detail: Updates Session Context with local session ID for subsequent message processing.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T01, T05: Store session ID in context

functions.name: set_peer_session_identifier
functions.description: Sets the Peer Session Identifier in the Session Context
functions.algorithm_detail: Updates Session Context with peer's session ID for message addressing.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T05, T06: Store peer session ID after receiving their message

functions.name: check_pbkdf_parameters_known
functions.description: Checks if PBKDF parameters (salt and iterations) are already known from out-of-band source
functions.algorithm_detail: Returns true if initiator has salt and iterations from QR code or other source, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T01: Determine HasPBKDFParameters value

functions.name: send_pbkdf_param_request

parameters.name: passcode_id
parameters.description: Passcode identifier (0 for current commissioning window)

functions.description: Constructs and sends PBKDFParamRequest message via Unsecured Session
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV-encodes pbkdfparamreq-struct with Protocol ID = PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, I Flag = 1. Session ID = 0, S Flag = 1, DSIZ = 0.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T01: Initiate PASE protocol

functions.name: construct_pbkdf_parameter_set

parameters.name: has_pbkdf_parameters
parameters.description: Whether initiator already has PBKDF params

functions.description: Constructs PBKDF parameter set for response, optionally including salt and iterations
functions.algorithm_detail: If has_pbkdf_parameters is true, returns empty parameter set. If false, includes: iterations (CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MIN to CRYPTO_PBKDF_ITERATIONS_MAX), salt (16-32 random bytes).
functions.usage_in_fsm: T05: Prepare PBKDF parameters for response

functions.name: compute_pake_values_initiator
functions.description: Computes Crypto_PAKEValues_Initiator (w0, w1) using PBKDF2
functions.algorithm_detail: CRYPTO_W_SIZE_BYTES = CRYPTO_GROUP_SIZE_BYTES + 8. w0s || w1s = Crypto_PBKDF(passcode_le32, salt, iterations, 2 * CRYPTO_W_SIZE_BITS). w0 = w0s mod p, w1 = w1s mod p, where p is the order of P-256.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T07: Initiator computes PAKE values before computing pA

functions.name: compute_pake_values_responder
functions.description: Computes Crypto_PAKEValues_Responder (w0, L) using PBKDF2 and elliptic curve multiplication
functions.algorithm_detail: Same as initiator for w0, w1. Then L = w1 * P where P is P-256 generator. w1 SHALL NOT be stored. Only (w0, L) retained as verifier.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T05: Responder computes PAKE verifier values

functions.name: Crypto_pA
functions.description: Computes SPAKE2+ prover's first message (pA point)
functions.algorithm_detail: pA computed per SPAKE2+ specification: pA = x*P + w0*M, where x is random scalar, P is P-256 generator, M is SPAKE2+ constant point. Output is uncompressed public key format per SEC 1 section 2.3.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T07: Initiator computes pA for Pake1

functions.name: Crypto_pB
functions.description: Computes SPAKE2+ verifier's first message (pB point)
functions.algorithm_detail: pB computed per SPAKE2+ specification: pB = y*P + w0*N, where y is random scalar, P is P-256 generator, N is SPAKE2+ constant point. Output is uncompressed public key format per SEC 1 section 2.3.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T09: Responder computes pB for Pake2

functions.name: Crypto_Transcript
functions.description: Computes SPAKE2+ transcript TT for key derivation
functions.algorithm_detail: Context = Crypto_Hash('CHIP PAKE V1 Commissioning' || PBKDFParamRequest || PBKDFParamResponse). TT = length(Context) || Context || 0x00...00 || 0x00...00 || length(M) || M || length(N) || N || length(pA) || pA || length(pB) || pB || length(Z) || Z || length(V) || V || length(w0) || w0. Per SPAKE2+ specification.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T09, T11, T12: Both parties compute transcript

functions.name: Crypto_P2
functions.description: Computes SPAKE2+ confirmation values and encryption key
functions.algorithm_detail: K_main || K_confirmP || K_confirmV = KDF(Hash(TT)). Ke = first 16 bytes of K_main. cA = HMAC(K_confirmP, pB), cB = HMAC(K_confirmV, pA). Ke is CRYPTO_HASH_LEN_BYTES/2 = 16 bytes.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T09, T11, T12: Compute confirmation values and shared secret

functions.name: derive_session_keys
functions.description: Derives I2RKey, R2IKey, and AttestationChallenge from Ke
functions.algorithm_detail: SEKeys_Info = 'SessionKeys' (0x53657373696F6E4B657973). I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=Ke, salt=[], info=SEKeys_Info, len=3*CRYPTO_SYMMETRIC_KEY_LENGTH_BITS). Each key is 128 bits.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T12, T15: Both parties derive session encryption keys

functions.name: send_pake1
functions.description: Constructs and sends Pake1 message
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV-encodes pake-1-struct with pA field. Protocol ID = PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, I Flag = 1.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T07: Send initiator's SPAKE2+ contribution

functions.name: send_pake2
functions.description: Constructs and sends Pake2 message
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV-encodes pake-2-struct with pB and cB fields. Protocol ID = PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, I Flag = 0.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T09: Send responder's contribution and confirmation

functions.name: send_pake3
functions.description: Constructs and sends Pake3 message
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV-encodes pake-3-struct with cA field. Protocol ID = PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, I Flag = 1.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T12: Send initiator's confirmation to responder

functions.name: send_status_report

parameters.name: protocol_data
parameters.description: Optional additional data (retry delay for BUSY)

functions.description: Sends Secure Channel Status Report message
functions.algorithm_detail: Encodes StatusReport per Section 4.11. For PASE success: GeneralCode=SUCCESS, ProtocolCode=SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS. For errors: GeneralCode=FAILURE, ProtocolCode=INVALID_PARAMETER.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T03, T04, T11, T14, T15: Send status responses

functions.name: get_current_timestamp
functions.description: Gets current timestamp for session age tracking
functions.algorithm_detail: Returns timestamp suitable for determining relative session age compared to other sessions.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T15, T16: Record SessionTimestamp

functions.name: initialize_message_counter
functions.description: Initializes Local Message Counter per Section 4.6.1.1
functions.algorithm_detail: Initializes message counter for replay protection. Initial value may be random or persisted.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T15, T16: Initialize message counters for secure session

functions.name: clear_session_state
functions.description: Clears all temporary session state variables
functions.algorithm_detail: Securely erases w0, w1, Ke, session keys, and other cryptographic material from memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T19, T20: Clean up after error

definitions.definition: Passcode-Authenticated Session Establishment - Protocol for establishing secure session using shared passcode via SPAKE2+ augmented PAKE
definitions.security_relevance: First session establishment between Commissioner and Commissionee during commissioning

definitions.definition: Augmented Password-Authenticated Key Exchange protocol where only one party (prover/initiator) knows the password, while verifier (responder) stores only a derived value
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents offline dictionary attacks even if verifier database is compromised

definitions.definition: Tuple (w0, w1) derived from passcode via PBKDF2, used by Commissioner to compute pA
definitions.security_relevance: Contains password-derived secrets; w1 used to compute pA and confirmation

definitions.definition: Tuple (w0, L) where L = w1*P, stored by Commissionee as verifier. w1 is never stored.
definitions.security_relevance: Augmented PAKE property: even if (w0, L) is stolen, offline dictionary attack not directly possible

definitions.definition: Encryption key derived from SPAKE2+ protocol, 128 bits, used to derive session keys
definitions.security_relevance: Shared secret from which I2RKey, R2IKey, AttestationChallenge are derived

definitions.definition: Protocol transcript including context prefix, PBKDF messages, pA, pB, intermediate values for key confirmation
definitions.security_relevance: Binds session keys to specific protocol run, prevents key substitution attacks

definitions.definition: Confirmation values: cA = HMAC(KcA, pB), cB = HMAC(KcB, pA), proving each party computed correct shared secret
definitions.security_relevance: Mutual authentication - both parties prove knowledge of same Ke

definitions.definition: Initiator-to-Responder encryption key, 128 bits, used by initiator to encrypt and responder to decrypt
definitions.security_relevance: Provides confidentiality for messages from Commissioner to Commissionee

definitions.definition: Responder-to-Initiator encryption key, 128 bits, used by responder to encrypt and initiator to decrypt
definitions.security_relevance: Provides confidentiality for messages from Commissionee to Commissioner

definitions.definition: 128-bit value derived alongside session keys, used only during device attestation procedure
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents replay of attestation responses across sessions

definitions.definition: Timestamp recorded when session is established, for determining relative session age
definitions.security_relevance: Enables session management and cleanup of stale sessions

definitions.definition: Session with Session ID = 0, used for PASE messages before secure session is established
definitions.security_relevance: Initial messages unencrypted but integrity protected by PAKE protocol itself

definitions.definition: Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2, used with HMAC-SHA256 to derive w0, w1 from passcode
definitions.security_relevance: Iteration count (1000-100000) slows brute-force attacks on weak passcodes

definitions.definition: Fixed points on P-256 curve used in SPAKE2+ protocol for blinding pA and pB computations
definitions.security_relevance: Must be nothing-up-my-sleeve points; values from SPAKE2+ draft specification

security_properties.description: Both parties are mutually authenticated via cA/cB verification: initiator proves knowledge of passcode, responder proves possession of matching verifier
Session keys (I2RKey, R2IKey) are secret from passive eavesdropper even if passcode is weak
Compromise of long-term passcode does not reveal past session keys due to ephemeral randomness in each SPAKE2+ run
Augmented PAKE (SPAKE2+) ensures that stealing responder's verifier (w0, L) does not enable offline dictionary attack
Fresh random nonces (InitiatorRandom, ResponderRandom) in each session prevent replay of old transcripts
Session keys bound to specific InitiatorSessionId and ResponderSessionId, preventing session hijacking
Invalid passcodeID or confirmation value mismatch results in error state, no session established
Initiator SHALL NOT send encrypted application data until PakeFinished received

GAP_001.description: Initiator does NOT validate PBKDF parameters from responder
GAP_001.spec_reference: Page 169 - No validation steps between PBKDFParamResponse receipt and Pake1
GAP_001.vulnerability: Malicious responder can set iterations=1, enabling offline passcode cracking in milliseconds

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Elliptic curve scalar multiplication on P-256
Elliptic curve scalar multiplication on P-256

cryptographic_operations.output: Z, V intermediate values for transcript
assumptions: CDH problem is hard on P-256

cryptographic_operations.output: I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge (48 bytes total, 16 bytes each)

security_assumptions.assumption: Passcode is obtained out-of-band securely by initiator
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker with passcode can impersonate commissioner

security_assumptions.assumption: Responder has pre-computed Crypto_PAKEValues_Responder (w0, L) before session starts
Crypto_DRBG produces cryptographically secure random numbers
security_assumptions.if_violated: Predictable nonces enable replay attacks and key recovery

security_assumptions.assumption: PBKDF2 iterations are sufficient to slow offline attacks (minimum 1000)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Fast offline dictionary attack possible with captured transcript

security_assumptions.assumption: Session identifiers are unique and non-overlapping with existing sessions
P-256 elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem is computationally hard
security_assumptions.if_violated: All elliptic curve operations compromised, complete break

security_assumptions.assumption: SPAKE2+ M and N points are nothing-up-my-sleeve values
Messages are delivered reliably (TCP or MRP)
Responder stores only (w0, L), never w1
security_assumptions.if_violated: Augmented PAKE security property lost; offline attack possible with w1

security_assumptions.assumption: Session keys are securely erased from memory after use
security_assumptions.if_violated: Memory disclosure attacks reveal session keys
